paraphrase: Euron possesses mysterious magical trappings -- the horn Dragonbinder, warlock 'slaves', and Valyrian steel armour -- linking him to Bloodraven, the Faceless Men, and Valyria.
Euron's ship the Silence has a mute crew with their tongues ripped out, black sails, and decks painted red to hide blood; it is feared all across the eastern seas from Ib to Asshai.

spoken_quote: and its name is Euron Crow's Eye
paraphrase: Victarion and Aeron hate and fear Euron even before he appears in the story; Aeron thinks of him in ominous terms.
verbatim: Aeron tugged his beard, and thought. I have seen the storm, and its name is Euron Crow’s Eye. “For now, send only silence,” he told the lord. “I must pray on this.”

paraphrase: In Book 3, King Balon dies suddenly, falling from a bridge on Pyke; Euron returns the very next day after years away and claims the throne. Asha thinks Euron murdered Balon.

spoken_quote: a drowned crow with seaweed hanging from his wings
paraphrase: Arya meets the witch called the ghost of High Heart, who has a vision of a faceless man on a swaying bridge with a drowned, seaweed-hung crow perched on his shoulder -- read as a Faceless Man assassin marked with Euron's symbol.
verbatim: Beside the embers of their campfire, she saw Tom, Lem, and Greenbeard talking to a tiny little woman, a foot shorter than Arya and older than Old Nan, all stooped and wrinkled and leaning on a gnarled black cane. Her white hair was so long it came almost to the ground. When the wind gusted it blew about her head in a fine cloud. Her flesh was whiter, the color of milk, and it seemed to Arya that her eyes were red, though it was hard to tell from the bushes. “The old gods stir and will not let me sleep,” she heard the woman say. “I dreamt I saw a shadow with a burning heart butchering a golden stag, aye. I dreamt of a man without a face, waiting on a bridge that swayed and swung. On his shoulder perched a drowned crow with seaweed hanging from his wings. I dreamt of a roaring river and a woman that was a fish. Dead she drifted, with red tears on her cheeks, but when her eyes did open, oh, I woke from terror. All this I dreamt, and more. Do you have gifts for me, to pay me for my dreams?”

paraphrase: It's implied that the Faceless Men can be paid with a meaningful personal sacrifice instead of money, especially for an important target.
Euron claims he once had a dragon egg, tried to make it hatch, got frustrated, and threw it into the sea.
Some fans believe Euron actually gave the dragon egg to the Faceless Men as payment for killing Balon, rather than really discarding it.

spoken_quote: blood eye
paraphrase: Euron's eyepatch symbolizes his hidden nature: his visible 'smiling eye' is charming and magnanimous, while the hidden eye behind the patch is his 'blood eye', where his cruelty, sorcery, and ambition live.

At the kingsmoot, Euron wins the crown through a mix of bribery, murder, and grandiose boasting about raiding, raping, and looting -- exactly the qualifications the ironborn respect -- while promising to restore their glorious past as conquerors.

Euron shows off his magic horn Dragonbinder at the kingsmoot, claiming it can bind dragons to his will, and announces a plan to sail to Meereen to steal Daenerys Targaryen's dragons and use them to conquer Westeros.

spoken_quote: When the kraken weds the dragon
paraphrase: Euron declares he wants 'the world', wants dragons and 'Woe', and plans to marry Daenerys.
verbatim: “So are the contents of my chamber pot. None is fit to sit the Seastone Chair, much less the Iron Throne. No, to make an heir that’s worthy of him, I need a different woman. When the kraken weds the dragon, brother, let all the world beware.”

paraphrase: Euron sends his brother Victarion, who hates him (Euron slept with Victarion's wife, leading Victarion to beat her to death, which caused Euron's exile), east with Dragonbinder to fetch Daenerys; Victarion secretly plans to take her for himself as revenge.

spoken_quote: blind to the tentacles that grasp
paraphrase: Euron knows about and exploits Victarion's resentment to manipulate him -- Victarion is described as a puppet, blind to the forces controlling him.
verbatim: “I have seen you in the nightfires, Victarion Greyjoy. You come striding through the flames stern and fierce, your great axe dripping blood, blind to the tentacles that grasp you at wrist and neck and ankle, the black strings that make you dance.”

paraphrase: Dragonbinder is said to bring dragons to the horn's true 'master', not whoever blows it -- and Euron, not Victarion, is that master, implying Victarion is doomed to fail at capturing a dragon for himself.
Fan speculation that Euron might ultimately steal the dragon Viserion himself, similar to how the Night King steals a dragon in the TV show.

spoken_quote: know unless we leap from some tall tower?
paraphrase: Euron says that as a boy he dreamed he could fly; when he woke, his maester told him he couldn't, but Euron questions whether the maester lied.
verbatim: Euron turned to face him, his bruised blue lips curled in a half smile. “Perhaps we can fly. All of us. How will we ever know unless we leap from some tall tower?” The wind came gusting through the window and stirred his sable cloak. There was something obscene and disturbing about his nakedness. “No man ever truly knows what he can do unless he dares to leap.”

paraphrase: This mirrors Bran Stark's story: Bran falls from a tower, then dreams of flying and is visited in the dream by the three-eyed crow (Bloodraven), who opens his third eye to magic; Bran's maester likewise says he can't do magic, and is wrong.
Bloodraven is called the 'three-eyed crow' and has one red eye; Euron is called the 'Crow's Eye' and his sigil is one red eye with crows -- a naming and symbol parallel.
Theory: Euron, like Bran, was visited in his dreams by the three-eyed crow (Bloodraven).

spoken_quote: the bones of a thousand other dreamers
paraphrase: In Bran's vision he sees the remains of many other dreamers Bloodraven tried to reach, suggesting Bloodraven auditioned many potential students who failed or died; theorized that Euron may have been one of these rejected students -- seen as too cruel to teach, but left with his third eye opened regardless.

Bloodraven and Bran are greenseers with the power to skinchange into other creatures' minds; it's unconfirmed whether Euron has this power, but the video argues it would make sense given the dream parallel.
Euron gives Victarion a mute 'dusky woman' companion for the eastward voyage; fans speculate she is spying for Euron, possibly via Euron skinchanging into her from afar.
Skinchanging a human is treated as an evil, abominable act in-world -- consistent with something Euron, who breaks every taboo, might be willing to do.
Skinchanging could explain how Euron coordinates his mute crew aboard the Silence, since the crew cannot speak to receive normal orders.
Some ironborn are said to be able to skinchange into sea creatures, showing the ability isn't exclusive to Starks/wargs -- supporting the idea that Euron could be a skinchanger too.

After Euron announces he'll marry Daenerys, she has an unsettling sex dream of a man with blue, bruised lips; Euron's own lips are blue and bruised from a drug he takes, implying he magically entered her dream.
Euron also enters Aeron's dreams later in the story, another instance of apparent dream-walking magic.

spoken_quote: The whole world
paraphrase: In Bran's flying dream he sees the whole world spread out below him -- Winterfell, Asshai, dragons, and the heart of winter -- and it's theorized Euron may have seen the same things in his own dream, explaining his hunger for total power.

spoken_quote: wonders and terrors beyond imagining
paraphrase: Euron says he sailed to Asshai by the Shadow, at the edge of the map, and saw wonders and terrors beyond imagining.
verbatim: “IRONMEN,” said Euron Greyjoy, “you have heard my horn. Now hear my words. I am Balon’s brother, Quellon’s eldest living son. Lord Vickon’s blood is in my veins, and the blood of the Old Kraken. Yet I have sailed farther than any of them. Only one living kraken has never known defeat. Only one has never bent his knee. Only one has sailed to Asshai by the Shadow, and seen wonders and terrors beyond imagining . . .”

paraphrase: Many of the story's most powerful sorcerers (Melisandre, Quaithe, Marwyn, Mirri Maz Duur) learned their magic in Asshai, framing it as a hub of dark power that Euron also drew from.
Near Asshai lies Stygai, the 'corpse city', feared even by shadowbinders; it's speculated Euron may have dared to enter it and found some evil power there.

Euron says he sailed to Valyria, the smoking demon-haunted ruins where it's said no one can survive -- and he does possess Valyrian steel armour and a Valyrian horn (Dragonbinder), suggesting he gained power there.
The old Valyrian empire used dragons and slavery to conquer the world, which may have inspired Euron's own ambitions of conquest.

Euron captured warlocks from Qarth (recently defeated by Daenerys) and took their 'shade of the evening', a psychedelic drug that gives visions of past and future and stains his lips blue and bruised.

spoken_quote: more truth in it than all the gods of earth
paraphrase: Euron says the shade 'opens his eyes' and that there's more truth in it than in all the gods of earth, reflecting his belief that gods and morality are lies.
Euron has murdered three of his own brothers -- Balon, and Harlon and Robin Greyjoy when they were young -- with kinslaying regarded as the ironborn's most abhorrent crime.

spoken_quote: None did
paraphrase: After choking the life from Harlon, Euron pissed into the sea and waited for a god to strike him down; none did, leading him to conclude that gods are lies and men are meat, feeling godlike for breaking every taboo with impunity.

spoken_quote: visit
paraphrase: Aeron's terror of Euron stems from childhood sexual abuse -- Euron says he 'taught Aeron how to pray', since Aeron would pray in fear whenever Euron came to 'visit' him at night.

spoken_quote: the godliest man ever to raise sail
paraphrase: Euron learned that sadistic cruelty inspires worship -- when he terrorizes the eastern seas aboard the Silence, men pray, and he calls himself the godliest man ever to raise sail.
verbatim: “—may sit the Seastone Chair, aye.” Euron glanced about the tent. “As it happens I have oft sat upon the Seastone Chair of late. It raises no objections.” His smiling eye was glittering. “Who knows more of gods than I? Horse gods and fire gods, gods made of gold with gemstone eyes, gods carved of cedar wood, gods chiseled into mountains, gods of empty air . . . I know them all. I have seen their peoples garland them with flowers, and shed the blood of goats and bulls and children in their names. And I have heard the prayers, in half a hundred tongues. Cure my withered leg, make the maiden love me, grant me a healthy son. Save me, succor me, make me wealthy . . . protect me! Protect me from mine enemies, protect me from the darkness, protect me from the crabs inside my belly, from the horselords, from the slavers, from the sellswords at my door. Protect me from the Silence.” He laughed. “Godless? Why, Aeron, I am the godliest man ever to raise sail! You serve one god, Damphair, but I have served ten thousand. From Ib to Asshai, when men see my sails, they pray.”

paraphrase: In the unreleased 'Forsaken' chapter (from the upcoming Winds of Winter, read publicly by George R. R. Martin at a convention), Euron imprisons and tortures Aeron physically and psychologically, forcing him to drink shade of the evening, which gives Aeron nightmarish visions of dead brothers and dead gods.

spoken_quote: These are the last days, when the world shall be broken and remade. A new god shall be born from the graves and charnel pits
paraphrase: In the Forsaken chapter, Euron declares that these are the last days, when the world shall be broken and remade, and that a new god shall be born from the graves and charnel pits.

spoken_quote: I am your god
paraphrase: Euron's hidden 'blood eye' is finally revealed in full horror, and he proclaims himself both king and god.

Euron imprisons priests of multiple religions -- priests of the Seven with their tongues ripped out, a red priest with a burned-off face, a warlock whose legs he rips off -- plus a girl named Falia, whom he seduced and impregnated with a promise of marriage before ripping out her tongue.
Euron ties Falia, Aeron, and the imprisoned priests to the front of his ships as he sails into battle against an enemy fleet, setting up a mass blood sacrifice.

spoken_quote: burning on a boiling blood-red sea
paraphrase: The coming battle is set to kill the imprisoned priests, Falia and her unborn child, Aeron, the enemy fleet, and Euron's own fleet in a catastrophic orgy of death; Aeron dreams of ironborn ships in the middle of it.

This mass-death ritual might 'summon krakens from the deep' -- in Aeron's vision krakens come at Euron's command, and it's established that krakens can be drawn to the surface by blood, matching the vision of a blood-red sea.

spoken_quote: A tall and twisted thing with one black eye and ten long arms, sailing on a sea of blood
paraphrase: The red priest Moqorro has a vision of Euron transformed into a monstrous being with one black eye and many long arms, sailing on a sea of blood.
verbatim: “Only their shadows,” Moqorro said. “One most of all. A tall and twisted thing with one black eye and ten long arms, sailing on a sea of blood.”

spoken_quote: his face a mass of writhing tentacles
paraphrase: Aeron sees Euron as no longer human, more squid than man, his face a mass of writhing tentacles.
Speculation that the Drowned God the ironborn worship might actually be an eldritch monster that Euron's blood sacrifice will awaken.
Alternative speculation that the dragon egg Euron threw into the sea earlier might hatch into a monstrous sea dragon as a result of all the blood magic.
Euron and his ironborn have been raiding towards Oldtown, and it's hinted his coming power will be turned against the city.

spoken_quote: towers by the sea, submerged beneath a black and bloody tide
paraphrase: Melisandre has a vision of towers by the sea, submerged beneath a black and bloody tide, rising from the depths -- read as Oldtown's Hightower under threat.
verbatim: “Some may.” Could the skulls in her vision have signified this bridge? Somehow Melisandre did not think so. “If it comes, that attack will be no more than a diversion. I saw towers by the sea, submerged beneath a black and bloody tide. That is where the heaviest blow will fall.”

paraphrase: Oldtown's Hightower is built on an ancient black-stone foundation which some legends say was built by fish-people ('Deep Ones'), linking it to Lovecraftian mythology.
The Citadel in Oldtown holds magical Valyrian artefacts called glass candles.
A Faceless Man has infiltrated the Citadel, possibly searching for a blood-soaked tome called 'The Death of Dragons'.

spoken_quote: raise an army from the deep
paraphrase: Oldtown is ruled by Leyton Hightower and his secluded 'Mad Maid', who have stayed in the Hightower for ten years consulting books of spells, possibly to raise an army from the deep.
verbatim: “To be sure. Lord Leyton’s locked atop his tower with the Mad Maid, consulting books of spells. Might be he’ll raise an army from the deeps. Or not. Baelor’s building galleys, Gunthor has charge of the harbor, Garth is training new recruits, and Humfrey’s gone to Lys to hire sellsails. If he can winkle a proper fleet out of his whore of a sister, we can start paying back the ironmen with some of their own coin. Till then, the best we can do is guard the sound and wait for the bitch queen in King’s Landing to let Lord Paxter off his leash.”

paraphrase: Legends tell of a Horn of Winter with the power to bring down the Wall; Mance Rayder claimed to have it, but it turned out to be fake.

In Book 2, Jon finds an old horn and gives it to Sam, who takes it to Oldtown; neither thinks it's important, but the text keeps subtly reminding readers Sam still has it, leading fans to suspect it is secretly the true Horn of Winter.
Speculation that Euron wants a White Walker apocalypse, since he says he wants to break the world and the Forsaken chapter is full of apocalyptic imagery.

spoken_quote: the heart of winter
paraphrase: Euron may have learned about the White Walkers during his childhood flying dream, paralleling how Bran's dream shows him the heart of winter at the end of the world.
Legend holds that the White Walkers will bring a Long Night that never ends.

spoken_quote: we shall feast before the fall of night
paraphrase: Euron is repeatedly described with imagery of night and darkness -- hair black as a midnight sea, sails black as a starless sky -- and says the ironborn shall feast before the fall of night.
verbatim: “I swore to give you Westeros,” the Crow’s Eye said when the tumult died away, “and here is your first taste. A morsel, nothing more . . . but we shall feast before the fall of night!” The torches along the walls were burning bright, and so was he, blue lips, blue eye, and all. “What the kraken grasps it does not loose. These isles were once ours, and now they are again . . . but we need strong men to hold them. So rise, Ser Harras Harlaw, Lord of Greyshield.” The Knight stood, one hand upon Nightfall’s moonstone pommel. “Rise, Andrik the Unsmiling, Lord of Southshield.” Andrik shoved away his women and lurched to his feet, like a mountain rising sudden from the sea. “Rise, Maron Volmark, Lord of Greenshield.” A beardless boy of six-and-ten years, Volmark stood hesitantly, looking like the lord of rabbits. “And rise, Nute the Barber, Lord of Oakenshield.”

paraphrase: At the kingsmoot, Euron's horn sounds three times; at the Night's Watch, three horn blasts is the traditional signal that White Walkers are coming.
Eastern legend holds that the first Long Night was caused by the Bloodstone Emperor.
The Bloodstone Emperor murdered his older sibling, the Amethyst Empress, to take her throne -- paralleling Euron murdering Balon to take his throne.
The Bloodstone Emperor practiced dark arts, torture, slavery, and cannibalism -- paralleling Euron's own practice of dark arts, torture, slavery, and cannibalism.

spoken_quote: cast down the true gods
paraphrase: The Bloodstone Emperor 'cast down the true gods', paralleling how Euron mocks and attacks the gods.
The Bloodstone Emperor worshipped a sinister black stone, paralleling the black stone of the ironborn throne, the Hightower's foundation, and the city of Asshai.
The Bloodstone Emperor married a 'tiger-woman', paralleling Euron's ambition to marry the 'dragon-woman' Daenerys.

spoken_quote: eyes are amethysts
paraphrase: The sister the Bloodstone Emperor usurped was called the Amethyst Empress; Euron himself points out that Daenerys's eyes are amethysts, a symbolic linguistic link.
Euron's hidden eye is called his 'blood eye', thematically echoing the name Bloodstone Emperor.

spoken_quote: history is a wheel
paraphrase: Rodrik the Reader states that history is a wheel and that what has happened before will happen again, framing Euron as history repeating the Bloodstone Emperor's role.
verbatim: “You may read it here. It is old and fragile.” He studied her, frowning. “Archmaester Rigney once wrote that history is a wheel, for the nature of man is fundamentally unchanging. What has happened before will perforce happen again, he said. I think of that whenever I contemplate the Crow’s Eye. Euron Greyjoy sounds queerly like Urron Greyiron to these old ears. I shall not go to Old Wyk. Nor should you.”

paraphrase: Fan theorist Poor Quentyn's theory: Euron will climb the Hightower in Oldtown and sound the Horn of Winter, breaking the Wall, bringing the Long Night, and sparking the apocalypse.

Thematic argument: Euron embodies 'silence', the opposite of the series' title A Song of Ice and Fire -- his ship is named Silence, he mutes his crew, and he silences his brothers through trauma; a sudden silence falls when he first enters the story.

spoken_quote: Those who follow me shall feast until the end of their days
paraphrase: Thematic argument: A Feast for Crows depicts only opportunists (the Bloody Mummers, Lady Stoneheart, Littlefinger, the Boltons, and Euron) benefiting from the war's chaos, with Euron declaring Westeros is dying and that his followers shall feast -- framing him as the title's literal 'Crow'.
Euron never personally swings a sword or casts a spell on-page -- his power comes entirely from exploiting people's fear, hope, and greed; he was chosen and 'democratically elected' at the kingsmoot.

The video raises its own doubt about Euron's claim to have simply thrown the dragon egg away, noting dragon eggs are extremely valuable and rare -- casting doubt on Euron's stated version before offering the Faceless-Men-payment alternative.

spoken_quote: not clear what exactly will happen
paraphrase: The video explicitly flags uncertainty about the Forsaken chapter's monstrous/eldritch imagery, saying it's 'not clear what exactly will happen' -- treating the Drowned-God-awakening and egg-hatches-into-a-sea-dragon ideas as open speculation rather than confirmed outcomes.
